strait.name: Strait of Hormuz
strait.closure_type: De facto (insurance-driven + active attacks on vessels)
strait.closure_method: Asymmetric denial via drone and missile strikes on commercial vessels, triggering insurance-driven shutdown. IRGC used sea drones, anti-ship missiles, and shore-launched projectiles to strike 21+ vessels since Feb 28. War risk insurance withdrawn or repriced at ~5% of vessel value — a 100x increase from pre-war 0.02-0.05%. For a $100M tanker, $5M per transit. At least one tanker paid $2M for transit rights through Iran-approved Larak Island corridor. Trump ordered DFC to provide $20B in political risk reinsurance (Chubb as lead underwriter), but JPMorgan estimates actual exposure at $352B across 329 tankers. Reports of naval mine deployment began ~March 10; US destroyed 44+ Iranian minelayers. Iran's stockpile estimated at 2,000-6,000 naval mines. Iran retains 80%+ of its small boats and minelayers. London Joint War Committee extended high-risk area to include waters around Oman.
strait.status: Functionally blockaded under selective Iranian control. Only ~77 vessels have crossed since March 1 (half shadow fleet). Iran operating de facto selective blockade — a vessel registration and vetting system allowing passage for friendly nations (China, India, Pakistan, Turkey, Japan under negotiation) while blocking US allies. Tanker traffic down over 90% from pre-war 100+ transits/day. Daily transits in single digits. JMIC/UKMTO threat level: CRITICAL ('attack almost inevitable'). 2,000-3,000 vessels and 20,000-40,000 seafarers stranded. War risk insurance at ~5% of ship value. 22 countries released joint statement condemning Iran's closure (Mar 21). Seven-nation Hormuz coalition (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, Netherlands, Canada — South Korea joining Mar 21) issued statement of 'readiness' but largely symbolic. Italian PM Meloni: no EU state considering military mission. France, Germany, Italy, Japan ruled out warships. US began aerial campaign to reopen Strait Mar 19 using A-10 Warthogs and Apache gunships. US Treasury temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships (Mar 21-Apr 19), expecting ~140M barrels to global supply.

impact.tankers_transiting_current_note: Only ~77 vessels have transited since March 1 versus pre-war 100+ ships/day. Shadow fleet comprising roughly half. Iran operating de facto selective blockade — friendly nations (China, India, Pakistan, Turkey, Japan under negotiation) allowed passage while blocking US allies. Iran exporting ~1.5 mbd to China uninterrupted. At least one tanker paid $2M for Larak Island corridor transit rights.
impact.insurance_cost_note: War-risk premiums surged from 0.02-0.05% to ~5% of vessel value — a 100x increase. For $100M tanker = $5M per transit. Major P&I clubs withdrew Gulf coverage by March 2. Trump: $20B DFC reinsurance (Chubb lead). JPMorgan: $352B actual exposure across 329 tankers. London Joint War Committee extended high-risk area to include Oman waters.

alternative_routes.name: East-West Pipeline (Saudi Petroline)
alternative_routes.status: Full capacity since March 11 per Aramco CEO Nasser. Effective Red Sea export via Yanbu ~4-5 mbd after domestic refinery consumption.

alternative_routes.name: IPSA Pipeline (Iraq-Saudi)
alternative_routes.status: Mothballed since 1990 — would need 2-3 months rehabilitation

alternative_routes.name: Habshan-Fujairah ADCOP (UAE)
alternative_routes.status: Intermittent — Fujairah attacked 4 times since Mar 9. Oil loadings suspended multiple times. Bunkering volumes down 60%+.

alternative_routes.name: Iraq-Kurdistan (Kirkuk-Ceyhan)
alternative_routes.status: Restarted March 18 at ~250,000 bpd. Potential 450,000 bpd. Only 6-13% of Iraq's pre-war Basra exports (3.4 mbd).

impact.shadow_tanker_transit_note: Shadow fleet ~50% of all transits. ~3 transits/day mostly Chinese-flagged and Iranian-permitted vessels. Iran operating selective passage system for friendly nations.
impact.vessels_attacked_note: 21+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28 per UKMTO. At least 8 maritime workers killed. JMIC/UKMTO threat level: CRITICAL ('attack almost inevitable'). Breakdown: bulk carriers, tankers, container ships, service vessels.
impact.kharg_island_strike_note: US struck 90+ military targets on Kharg Island Day 14-15. Oil infrastructure deliberately spared.
impact.south_pars_strike_note: Israeli Air Force struck South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 and Asaluyeh hub with US coordination. First upstream energy strike. ~100M cm³/day gas offline. Two refineries halted. Qatar condemned.
impact.natanz_strike_note: Joint US-Israeli strike on Natanz nuclear enrichment facility March 21. IAEA confirmed no radiation increase. DG Grossi called for 'military restraint.' Russia condemned. Israel denied responsibility. Second Natanz strike in Operation Epic Fury.
impact.hormuz_aerial_campaign_note: US began aerial campaign to reopen Strait March 19 using A-10 Warthogs and Apache gunships against Iranian fast-attack boats, drones, and coastal positions.
impact.iran_selective_passage_note: Iran operating de facto selective blockade — vessel registration and vetting system. Friendly nations (China, India, Pakistan, Turkey, Japan under negotiation) allowed passage. Iran exporting ~1.5 mbd to China uninterrupted. At least one tanker paid $2M for Larak Island corridor transit. US allies blocked.
impact.us_escort_status: No operational escort capability in Strait yet. USS Tripoli approaching Arabian Sea, ETA Mar 22-23. USS Boxer left San Diego Mar 19, ETA early April. Combined: ~4,400 Marines. US began aerial campaign to reopen Strait Mar 19 (A-10s, Apaches). CRITICAL MCM GAP: USS Tulsa/Santa Barbara in Singapore — MCM technology works ~30% of time per CSM. 22 countries condemn closure. Seven-nation coalition 'readiness' statement largely symbolic. Italian PM Meloni: no EU state considering military mission. France, Germany, Italy, Japan ruled out warships. South Korea joined coalition Mar 21.
impact.us_sanctions_lift_note: US Treasury temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships (effective Mar 21 until Apr 19), expecting ~140M barrels to global supply. Iran dismissed, saying it has no surplus crude to sell.
impact.irgc_threat_march_18: IRGC issued 'urgent warning' following South Pars strike — named SAMREF Refinery (Saudi), Jubail Petrochemical Complex (Saudi), Al Hosn Gas Field (UAE), Mesaieed (Qatar), Ras Laffan (Qatar) as 'direct and legitimate targets in the coming hours.' Urged civilian/worker evacuation.
impact.ruwais_refinery_status: Shut — Abu Dhabi's largest refinery (922,000 bpd) offline after earlier drone strikes.
impact.day_22_events: Day 22 — Nowruz/Eid — no operational pause. 8,000+ US targets, 8,000+ sorties, 130 vessels destroyed. Natanz struck. 5,000-lb bunker-busters on Hormuz coastal sites. Aerial campaign continues (A-10s, Apaches). Diego Garcia attacked from 2,500mi. Dimona struck. 9 salvos at Israel. 22 countries condemn closure. South Korea joins coalition. US lifts sanctions on loaded Iranian oil. Brent ~$108. S&P 6,506 (6-month low). Goldman warns could exceed $147. JPMorgan: 'policy measures may have limited impact unless safe passage assured.'
